title: Map errors to HTTP status codes
prompt_md:
A central error handler turns an exception's name into an HTTP status code. Write `error_to_status(exc_name)` that returns:

- `400` for `"ValueError"`
- `403` for `"PermissionError"`
- `404` for `"KeyError"`
- `500` for anything else (the catch-all "server broke" code)

This is the glue between business-logic errors and what the HTTP layer reports.
hints: A dict mapping name -> code is cleaner than a chain of ifs.
Use `dict.get(exc_name, 500)` so unknown names fall through to 500.
